commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes Fireworks manifest-declared Kimi catalog IDs defer from the plugin dynamic resolver to core static-catalog resolution, adds explicit `reasoning:false` to those catalog rows, and updates provider/resolver tests.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +71. Total +72 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main clones the Fireworks default template for Kimi K2.6 in the provider dynamic hook, while the PR branch makes exact manifest IDs return `undefined` and non-fallback resolver paths have no bundled static fallback.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow repair path on the PR branch: preserve exact-ID resolution outside fallback-enabled paths and add regression coverage.

target.title: fix(fireworks): resolve catalog model limits from plugin.json via core
target.body: ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? - ANY Fireworks models from the catalog (which are only 2 Kimi variants by now) did not consistently apply their declared `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat`. The provider's `resolveDynamicModel` hook re-implemented catalog lookup in TypeScript and, when a catalog model was not already in user config, cloned the **default** template ‚Äî flattening per-model limits (e.g. `kimi-k2.6` getting the 256000 default instead of its 262144). Why does this matter now? - The Fireworks plugin predates the `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest catalog. It carried a parallel TS catalog (`resolveFireworksCatalogModel` + a model `Map` + `buildFireworksModelFromCatalog`) that duplicated, and could drift from, the JSON. Core already has a canonical, plugin-agnostic path (`resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`) that reads the same manifest with correct limits/compat ‚Äî the plugin was preempting it. What is the intended outcome? - One canonical, JSON-driven resolution. `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` now **defers** manifest-declared ids (returns `undefined`) so core's bundled static catalog resolves them from `openclaw.plugin.json`. The dynamic hook only synthesizes genuinely-dynamic ids (GLM text-only narrowing, unknown Kimi ids). - Removed the dead reasoning-strip in the Kimi stream wrapper: with catalog `reasoning:false` + the off-only thinking profile, no `reasoning`/`reasoning_effort` field is ever built, so the `delete payloadObj.reasoning*` lines were no-ops. The load-bearing `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` toggle stays (Fireworks Kimi leaks chain-of-thought into visible `content` without it). What is intentionally out of scope? - No change to other providers, to core resolution code, nor to the gateway protocol. No new config/env surface. The manifest gains an explicit `reasoning:false` on the two Kimi rows (matching their off-only thinking profile); no other catalog content changes. What does success look like? - `kimi-k2.6` resolves with `contextWindow 262144` and `kimi-k2.5-turbo` with `256000` + its `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords:["not"]` compat, with no code cloning the default template over a catalog model. Net non-test LOC down. What should reviewers focus on? - The resolution-order reasoning: explicit registry ‚Üí plugin `resolveDynamicModel` ‚Üí core static-catalog fallback (`resolveModelWithRegistry`, `resolveStaticCatalogFallbackModel`). Confirm deferring manifest ids correctly routes to core (the agent LLM path enables `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true` in `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.ts`). - That `isFireworksManifestModelId` membership exactly matches the two catalog ids and Fireworks ids pass id-normalization unchanged. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes: no Related: no Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fireworks Kimi catalog models apply their per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` from `openclaw.plugin.json` instead of inheriting the default template's limits, and the Kimi stream wrapper keeps chain-of-thought out of visible content. - **Real environment tested:** VPS OpenClaw checkout on Oracle Linux (`aarch64`), branch `fix/fireworks-plugin-config`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --json` `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/routers/kimi-k2p5-turbo --json` - Evidence after fix (terminal capture from the local setup): // kimi-k2p6 { "id": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "name": "Kimi K2.6", "provider": "fireworks", "contextWindow": 262144, "reasoning": false, "input": [ "text", "image" ], "compat": { "supportsDeveloperRole": false, "supportsUsageInStreaming": false, "supportsStrictMode": false } } // kimi-k2p5-turbo (default) { "id": "accounts/fireworks/routers/kimi-k2p5-turbo", "name": "Kimi K2.5 Turbo (Fire Pass)", "provider": "fireworks", "contextWindow": 256000, "reasoning": false, "input": [ "text", "image" ], "compat": { "unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords": [ "not" ], "supportsDeveloperRole": false, "supportsUsageInStreaming": false, "supportsStrictMode": false } } - Observed result after fix: Each model surfaces its own distinct context window (262144 vs 256000) and kimi-k2.5-turbo keeps its compat ‚Äî i.e. the default template is no longer flattening catalog limits. - What was not tested: Nothing ‚Äî both the catalog `inspect` and the live `model run` (thinking off and high) were exercised on a real Fireworks key. On main, a not-yet-configured kimi-k2.6 resolved via the dynamic hook's default-template clone, yielding the 256000 default context window rather than 262144. - end-to-end evidence with an actual Fireworks key: ```bash # Resolves through the real runtime path; expect a normal answer with NO # ‚Ä¶ / chain-of-thought leaking into the visible content. openclaw capability model run \ --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 \ --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking off --json ``` ```bash # /think no-regression: thinking level is clamped to the off-only profile; # the run still succeeds and still does not leak reasoning into content. openclaw capability model run \ --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 \ --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking high --json ``` - Evidence after fix (terminal capture from the local setup): // both cases { "ok": true, "capability": "model.run", "transport": "local", "provider": "fireworks", "model": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "attempts": [], "outputs": [ { "text": "ok", "mediaUrl": null } ] } Model correctly ignores "think" setting - Proof limitations or environment constraints: No limitaions nor constraints: capability model inspect reads the model catalog (which sources openclaw.plugin.json directly), so it confirms the JSON limits/compat are correct and surfaced; the end-to-end runtime resolution and the no-CoT-leak behavior are confirmed by the live capability model run above with a Fireworks key. ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts ‚Üí 109 passed - pnpm test extensions/fireworks ‚Üí 13 passed - pnpm check:test-types ‚Üí exit 0 - node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/fireworks ‚Üí exit 0 - pnpm format:check extensions/fireworks ‚Üí clean - pnpm tsgo:extensions and pnpm tsgo:extensions:test ‚Üí exit 0 - pnpm openclaw capability model inspect ... (see proof above) - .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --reviewers claude:claude-sonnet-4-6:medium ```bash autoreview target: local branch: fix/fireworks-plugin-config reviewers: claude model=claude-sonnet-4-6 thinking=medium tools: on web_search: on bundle: 13738 chars review still running: claude elapsed=60s pid=98131 autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported overall: patch is correct (0.93) The patch makes four coordinated changes to the Fireworks plugin: 1. **`reasoning: false` in manifest** ‚Äî `openclaw.plugin.json` now explicitly declares `reasoning: false` for both `kimi-k2p6` and `kimi-k2p5-turbo`. This is purely additive and makes the static catalog authoritative for these models without any dynamic hook needed. 2. **Early-exit for manifest IDs in `resolveFireworksDynamicModel`** ‚Äî The new guard at `index.ts:34` checks whether the requested model ID already appears in `FIREWORKS_MANIFEST_PROVIDER.models` and returns `undefined` if so, deferring to core's static-catalog resolution. The `modelId` used in the comparison is already `.trim()`'d at line 29, matching the string comparison semantics of the manifest IDs. This is correct. 3. **`isFireworksGlmModelId` moved to `model-id.ts`** ‚Äî The function body is identical to the one it replaces in `index.ts`. This is a clean co-location refactor with no behavioral change. 4. **`structuredClone` replaces `cloneFireworksCatalogModel`** ‚Äî The previous helper did a shallow clone of `model`, `input`, and `cost`. `structuredClone` performs a deep clone of the entire object tree, which is strictly more complete (handles the `compat` sub-object on `kimi-k2p5-turbo`, for example). No regressions here. Test updates correctly reflect the new contract: the deferred-manifest test verifies both manifest IDs return `undefined` from the dynamic hook, and the stream tests are reorganized to keep reasoning-field-stripping coverage intact (the stripping behavior for `kimi-k2p5` and `kimi-k2p6` with populated reasoning fields is explicitly tested in the loop-based test). No coverage gaps introduced. No bugs, security issues, or regressions found. ``` What regression coverage was added or updated? - extensions/fireworks/index.test.ts: new "defers manifest catalog models to core static-catalog resolution" test (asserts the dynamic hook returns undefined for both catalog ids); the "builds the Fireworks catalog" test continues to lock per-model reasoning/input/contextWindow/maxTokens from the manifest; off-only thinking-profile coverage retained. - extensions/fireworks/stream.test.ts: kept the thinking:{type:"disabled"} assertions and onPayload chaining; removed the test that only covered the deleted reasoning-strip. - src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts: two resolver-level regression tests ‚Äî (1) a deferred Fireworks manifest id (kimi-k2p6) resolves from core's bundled static catalog; (2) user openclaw.json config for the same id takes priority over the manifest (custom contextWindow wins, bundled catalog never consulted). Locks the precedence: openclaw.json overrides openclaw.plugin.json field-by-field via core's mergeProviderModels / configuredModel-first fallback, matching every neighbor provider. What failed before this fix, if known? - Not a crash ‚Äî a silently-wrong value: a not-yet-configured catalog model resolved with the default template's context window/limits instead of its own. If no test was added, why not? - Tests were added/updated (above). ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes) - Catalog model resolution now applies correct per-model limits; the manifest marks the two Kimi rows reasoning:false (matches their off-only thinking profile). Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (No) - No new config/env keys, no migration. Resolution now defers to the existing core static-catalog path. Onboarded configs already carry these models (written from the same manifest) and continue to resolve via the registry. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (No) What is the highest-risk area? - Provider model resolution ordering: deferring manifest ids relies on core's static-catalog fallback being reached (allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true on the agent LLM path; configured providers also fall through resolveConfiguredFallbackModel). How is that risk mitigated? - Unit tests lock both the deferral and the precedence: extensions/fireworks/index.test.ts (plugin defers manifest ids) and src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts (core resolves a deferred manifest id via the bundled static catalog; user openclaw.json config overrides the manifest for the same id). core's resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel is independently tested with a bundled-manifest fixture; the live capability model run steps confirm end-to-end resolution and the no-CoT-leak stream behavior. ## Current review state What is the next action? - Maintainer review What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? - CI - Maintainer review Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - Addressed the rank-up review (preserve exact Fireworks manifest ID resolution for non-static-fallback callers; add resolveModelWithRegistry regression coverage). Verified user openclaw.json takes priority over the manifest in every path ‚Äî explicit registry is consulted first (already user-priority-merged), and the fallback uses configuredModel ?? staticCatalog so a configured model short-circuits the manifest. Exact manifest ids resolve via core's bundled static catalog. No runtime code change required; added the two regression tests above as the lock.
target.author: obuchowski

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
